project_identity.name: Buiry
project_identity.description: BUild context memoRY — Developer infrastructure for persistent AI agent memory and dataset harvesting. MCP server + ADK agents + React dashboard.
project_identity.architecture_summary: MCP server (npx buiry-mcp) for local session memory via JSON. ADK multi-agent system calling MCP tools via stdio. React dashboard for visualization. Dataset SDK for interaction harvesting.

decisions_log.decision: Local-only for hackathon (no cloud backend)
decisions_log.reason: MCP server reads/writes local Build-Context-Memory.json. No cloud API, MemWal, or Redis needed for demo.

decisions_log.decision: Use subagents for parallel task execution
decisions_log.reason: Phase 1 has 7 independent tasks that can be parallelized. Subagents for MCP server, React scaffold, README, layout, dashboard.

sessions.last_session_summary: Project initialized with base AI starter and memory files. PRD/Arch templates not yet authored.
changes_made: Created ARCHITECTURE.md with MCP server component diagram

file_module_map.purpose: System architecture diagram and component descriptions
Detailed MCP tool signatures, input schemas, and output formats

decisions_log.decision: Use 7 MCP tools instead of a monolithic save/load API
decisions_log.reason: Fine-grained tools allow AI agents to compose workflows. start_session and end_session bracket work; checkpoint enables mid-session snapshots.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Single buiry_save tool with mode parameter

decisions_log.decision: Use stdio transport for MCP server
decisions_log.reason: Standard for local MCP servers. Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot without network config.

Write AI_Starter.md with comprehensive agent rules
Define the 5-phase build plan with milestones

sessions.last_session_summary: MCP server architecture defined with 7 tools. ARCHITECTURE.md and tool specs authored.
changes_made: Created schemas/build-context-memory.schema.json (JSON Schema draft-07)
Defined SessionObject type: session_id, timestamp, ai_agent, current_phase, progress, changes_made, file_module_map, decisions_log, known_issues, errors_encountered, next_steps, notes
Added $schema field to Build-Context-Memory.json for validation

file_module_map.purpose: JSON Schema v1 for Build-Context-Memory validation
Updated with $schema reference

decisions_log.decision: Use JSON Schema draft-07 instead of draft-04
decisions_log.reason: Better support for if/then/else conditional validation. Widely supported by ajv and Zod-to-JSON-Schema.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: JSON Schema draft-04, TypeScript interfaces only

decisions_log.decision: Store file_module_map as array of objects instead of a flat record
decisions_log.reason: Preserves insertion order and allows last_modified timestamps per file. Easier to sort by recency in the dashboard.

known_issues.issue: Schema doesn't yet support multi-agent session merging

next_steps: Write AI_Starter.md with 9 comprehensive agent rules
Define the 5-phase build plan with clear milestones
Scaffold buiry-mcp TypeScript project
sessions.notes: Schema v1 locked. All fields typed and validated. Ready for agent rules.

completed: Wrote AI_Starter.md with 9 mandatory rules for AI agents
Defined session lifecycle protocol (read → work → write → summarize)
Added error handling and rollback procedures
in_progress: Testing rules with a dry-run session

sessions.last_session_summary: JSON schema v1 created. Session object structure and validation finalized.
changes_made: Created AI_Starter.md with 9 rules: (1) Always read memory first, (2) Append-only writes, (3) Log every decision, (4) Checkpoint on major changes, (5) Reference files by path, (6) Summarize on session end, (7) Flag blockers immediately, (8) Never overwrite sessions, (9) Validate JSON before write
Added session lifecycle protocol: read → work → write → summarize
Defined error recovery: if write fails, log error to known_issues before retry

file_module_map.purpose: 9 mandatory rules for AI agent behavior during Buiry sessions
Detailed agent lifecycle protocol with examples

decisions_log.decision: Enforce 9 rules in AI_Starter.md as mandatory behavior
decisions_log.reason: Rules ensure consistent session quality across different AI agents. Rule 8 (never overwrite) prevents the #1 data loss risk.

known_issues.issue: Dry-run test not yet performed with actual MCP tools

decisions_log.decision: Use ESM modules (type: module in package.json)
decisions_log.reason: Modern Node.js standard. MCP SDK v1.12+ requires ESM. Avoids dual CJS/ESM complexity.

decisions_log.decision: Separate tool handlers into individual files under src/tools/
decisions_log.reason: Keeps each tool self-contained. Easier to test independently and add new tools without touching index.ts.

file_module_map.purpose: Zod validation schemas for all MCP tool inputs
buiry_get_context: returns filtered session history
Updated with JSON corruption recovery

decisions_log.decision: Use Zod for schema validation instead of manual checks
decisions_log.reason: Type-safe, co-pilot compatible, auto-generates TypeScript types. Single source of truth for input shapes.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Manual if-checks, ajv JSON Schema validation

decisions_log.decision: Make next_steps required in buiry_end_session
decisions_log.reason: Forces agents to plan ahead. Prevents empty sessions with no forward momentum. Core Buiry principle.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Optional next_steps with default empty array

decisions_log.decision: Use Tailwind CSS v4 (not v3) for Stitch token integration
decisions_log.reason: v4 has native CSS variable support, making Stitch design tokens easier to map. Better dark mode handling.

known_issues.issue: Tailwind v4 config format changed — PostCSS plugin approach differs from v3 docs
